recommended_claim_shape: HoloVerify completed a second committed 20-pair / 40-packet AP replication family. Holo solved 40/40 packets and 20/20 sibling pairs. Same-family one-shot solo baselines completed 120/120 calls and produced 53/120 KNEW/admissible outputs. Every AP pair had at least two strict solo failures across six one-shot attempts, while Holo solved both siblings. One AP pair showed complete all-six solo collapse. Holo used about 2.84x the solo token budget and passed packet-identity and no-leakage audits.

whitepaper_target.section: 09. What the benchmark has shown so far
whitepaper_target.placement: after Kit A / Kit B HoloVerify discussion and before HoloBuild

cumulative_safe_claim: Across two committed internal HoloVerify families, Holo solved 80/80 frozen action-boundary packets and 40/40 sibling pairs. The two families had different solo-baseline behavior: Kit C produced broad clean solo collapse, while AP produced mixed solo behavior with strict failures present in every pair.
forbidden_claims: AP proves Holo always beats solo.
Every solo failure was a wrong verdict.
Parse failures are the same as wrong business reasoning.
